assessment_items.question_content:
The pet store sells dogs, cats, and hamsters.

This bar graph shows how many of each pet are at the store.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many cats are there?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] cats
hints: First, find the bar that is for cats.

Next, find the number that is even with the top of the $\red{\text{Cat}}$ bar.

This number tells us how many cats there are.

$~$

[[☃ image 1]]

$~$

options.alt:
A vertical bar graph. Type of Pet is on
the horizontal axis. Number of Each is on the vertical axis and has lines marking from 0 to 10. There are bars for each of the Pets: 1 bar for Dog, 1 bar for Cat, and 1 bar for Hamster.
•Dog, 6. 
•Cat, 8.
• Hamster, 3.

assessment_items.question_content:
The weather in November was sunny, cloudy, or rainy.

This bar graph shows the  number of days of each kind of weather.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many rainy days were there in November?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] days
hints: First, find the bar that is for rainy weather.

Next, find the number that is even with the top of the $\red{\text{Rainy}}$ bar.

This number tells us how many days were rainy.

$~$

[[☃ image 1]]

$~$
There were $\red5$ rainy days in November.

options.alt:
A vertical bar graph. Weather is on
the horizontal axis. Days in November is on the vertical axis and has lines marking from 0 to 18. There are bars for each of the Weather: 1 bar for Sunny, 1 bar for Cloudy, and 1 bar for Rainy.
•Sunny, 18. 
•Cloudy, 7.
• Rainy, 5.

assessment_items.question_content:
The music store sells trumpets, flutes, and drums.

This bar graph shows how many of each instrument are at the store.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many *more* trumpets are there than flutes?**  

 [[☃ input-number 1]] more trumpets
hints: There are $11$ trumpets because the bar above the "trumpets" is $11$ units high.
There are $4$ flutes because the bar above "flutes" is $4$ units high.
There are $\blueD7$ more trumpets than flutes.

options.alt:
A vertical bar graph.  Number of Instruments is on the vertical axis and has lines marking from 0 to 14. There are bars for 3 instruments: 1 bar for Trumpet, 1 bar for Flute, and 1 bar for Drum.
•Trumpet, 11. 
•Flute, 4.
• Drum, 14.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mr. Z's class, Mrs. S's class, and Ms. R's class bought hot lunches on Tuesday.

This bar graph shows the number of hot lunches bought by each class.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many *fewer* hot lunches did Mrs. S's class buy than Mr. Z's class?**

 [[☃ input-number 1]] hot lunches
hints:
Mr. Z's class bought $\goldD{13}$ hot lunches.  
$~$

[[☃ image 1]]

$~$

Mrs. S's class bought $\greenD{8}$ hot lunches.  
$~$

[[☃ image 1]]

$~$
Mrs. S's class bought $\blueD5$ fewer hot lunches than Mr. Z's class.

A vertical bar graph. Teacher's Class is on
the horizontal axis. Hot Lunches is on the vertical axis and has lines marking from 0 to 15. There are bars for each of the Teacher's Classes: 1 bar for Mr. Z, 1 bar for Mrs. S, and 1 bar for Ms. R.
•Mr. Z, 13. 
•Mrs. S, 8.
• Ms. R, 11.
